C[dmg: Until there everything was ok.

C[dmg: Quite a strange move. You usually want to either live in the corner (in this case you go to R3) or escape by playing P6.

You can also double pincer at O3.

R4 doesn't really give eyes space and let white get the corner at R3.
MysticXG: oh... i see, i usually have a lot of trouble figuring out what to do in this situation.

C[dmg: This is slow but a consequence of R4.
A possibility here is to cut at Q3 but it's very fighty.

C[dmg: This move help white constructing on the fourth line. While your taking no point your giving white a strong territory.
However, it's not bad if you want to put pressure on R8

C[dmg: So instead of putting pressure, you're helping white to install.
Q10/P10 Would be move to consider (they putting pressure on R8 and constructing with R16).

C[dmg: This is slow and not needed.
Pincer around Q12 is still possible
C[dmg: White is happy to have this move.
C[dmg: I think that's either too far from the corner, or to close. Consider N16 or J17.
C[dmg: This is a bit passive. You can hane at B17 or cut at D17. I think hane is a bit better as it does not leave white in an easy position.
C[dmg: A bit passive. J16 and E17 might have been better.

C[dmg: Until now the game is pretty balanced. However, white has  way more opportunities to extend it's territory than black. Black should become invasive. A good idea is to take C3 but might be aji-killing. I would go for J3 here as you can extends on both direction and settle on the bottom.
C[dmg: This move pincer with a pincer... It's bad as you'll have two weak group really soon (D13 and D8).

C[dmg: Never redo that again (well there might be exception but here it's not sente).

To escape you do a one-space jump toward the center.
To settle you try to tak as much space as you can.
As you can't take a two-space jump on the border, you can't really escape or it will be painfull. The best is to keep that stones as aji. Invade the bottom.

C[dmg: Too passive I think. your entering into white peace.
Now white is taking a big corner and you have no eyes space.
Instead:
E4 is very fighty but helps your stones. E3 is putting pressure and keeping aji.
C[dmg: A bit late. Now black don't really have ideal extensions but for white, attacking this stones will help attacking the D6 group.
C[dmg: Same thing as for D6. Here you can extend to N3.
C[dmg: Threatening two groups at once.
C[dmg: However, this group is the safest. Then you should help the group on the bottom.
C[dmg: Now the only chance for K3 group is to escape using J4.

C[dmg: This is not making two eyes.  To make life you should always try to extends your group first. J2 then O2 can make a good basis for life however. I doubt black will get two eyes here. So J4 is probably the best now.
C[dmg: Now black is dead. And white gained a huge thickness that is going to be used for attacking every weak group (D6 then C13).

C[dmg: Yeah probably the best idea.
F7 Is a better shape but F8 is still ok.
C[dmg: White is continuing is pressuring. And black escape. Normal.
C[dmg: White was trying to cut. But you should have for the other group as it is the weakest. J9 was probably the best since the group can still escape even if white cut.
C[dmg: Black is cutted and is very weak.

C[dmg: And that's the worse error of all the game. You're playing a passive move to help an alive group while there is a group that is nearly dead.

D9 would have probably been the best move to live and J9 the best to fight.

C[dmg: Now black is close to death. It cannot escape because of G9. E7 will give at best one eye (rabit-6).
And black has not time to get both E7 and bottom eye.

C[dmg: Thanks again for the game. You should work on spotting classical life and death shape.

Also learn to be less submissive that will make your play much more interesting for you and you'll make much more points without even realizing it.
MysticXG: Thanks, I learned a lot

C[dmg: D6 here is more severe and it's uncutable.
C[dmg: What I've made (this diagonal jump) is called a elephant eye. It's a bad shape since it's very easy to cut. You should have played at F6. Separating and connecting your stones.
C[dmg: See, now you cut but you would not have played G6 now.
C[dmg: This is heavy and your group is not getting more liberties. However, this bad shape (the square), comes from the empty triangle formed by F6 which comes from G6.
C[dmg: This is a good idea to reduce white liberties. But you should go to the string with less liberties (H6 getting an eye in the corner. After that even if white goes to H3, you can go to H4)
C[dmg: Now the corner group has die. (2 liberties against 3)
C[dmg: Highly inefficient. You are playing inside your territory. If you want to protect you can go to D8.

C[dmg: 1/ two empty triangle.
2/ Not sente (while you have a sente move at D8)
3/ E3 is threatening the cut at E4, you should protect.
C[dmg: Again you are reducing the liberties of a group that is now uncapturable. You should reduce the liberties of E3 group. (Maybe F2 is working).
C[dmg: Again you tenuki for 0 point.
C[dmg: Now the three corner stones are dead.
C[dmg: There was no need for this stone. If white try to cut at C4 then you atari at B4 (and if it goes to B4 you connect at C4). There's no room for a living group inside.

dmg: If you use c7 as a ko threat I have tons of ko threat around C3. Basically, you had no chance of winning that ko.

C[Uberdude: Rather odd choice of direction to go into a tight spot like this. High is also not so good for territory. More usual would be r14 or d13.
cook777: I seen this played at E16. (Butlatter in the game.) Maybe he was thinking of that?
C[Uberdude: Perhaps e6 is ok here to build some influence which would work with j15 to make a moyo and harder for w to come into top left. 3-3 is ok too.
C[Uberdude: This is wrong side to block as w doesn't have an extension around k4 yet. This joseki is gote for white so black will get to split around k3.
C[Uberdude: I wonder about r11 instead.

C[Uberdude: Hard to know what to do as black, which is why I suggest r11 not r10. o14 is not 100% alive so could try aiming at a splitting attack, but it has decent shape and b has a lot to worry about with r10.
C[Uberdude: q12 is probably best to stay connected.
C[kaput: W may need to guard h2.
dmg: Well I've read it several time and I found nothing on H2. Even with the cuts at H6 and E5.
Then I don't see anything... G4 is answered by H3 for a local seki (throw in at G5). G5 is answered by H3 also. H3 is simply answered by G4. I really don't see much.
kaput: the seki may not be local.
w throw in at g5, then g6,f6,g5. after that, no matter what w plays, b e3.
dmg: Oh sorry, but b E3 doesn't work. As white cut and there's local seki. But you're right, black can simply connect for a seki. GRrrrrrr I missed it but I was looking at it for so long...
kaput: E3 doesn't work? We are probably not looking at the same line of moves
dmg: wH3/wG3 cuts local seki. Well It's not the problem. After H5, w does H4 that what I've seen during the game.
